title: A-Math but its minecraft
body: How to play: 1. Players must draw 1 pawn per side in order to see which side has played first. The digits are arranged in descending order of numbers. All symbols are considered lower than 0 and BLANK is the closest, whoever is close to 20 will play first. 2. Players pick up 8 pawns per side and place them on the keyboard where the players first catch them first. It takes no more than 2 minutes per time. 3. Players who have started playing must arrange the numbers into an equation in one way or another (for example, 7x2 = 5+9 or 7*7=42+7 or 6=6). Place them on the board horizontally or vertical only There must be one of the pawns on top of the star slot in the middle of the board. The pawn that overlaps the star slot will get a score of 3 times because the star in the middle of the board is a blue channel. 4. The first player must pick up the pawns in the bag equal to the number of used pawns. Then it will be the second player's turn. which must connect the existing pawns into an equation with at least one of the newly added pawns touching the existing pawns on the board, possibly increasing the number of numbers in the existing equation For example, Mr. A down 3+4 = 7, Mr. B may play 9-3+4 = 7+3, can be considered as a new equation. Calculating Score: 1. From the 4 special score fields, which are red channel (both equation x3), yellow channel (both equation x2), blue channel (multiply 3 only the one that overlaps the channel) and the orange channel (multiply 2 only the one that overlaps the channel) If a player enters an equation that has a new number of pawns that have been replayed over more than 1 extra slot, then the score will be counted for each extra point first and then taken into the extra box of the whole equation. For example, when a player Play 7-7 = 6x0 The number 7 is worth 2 points, superimposed on the blue box (only multiplier 3 ), symbol = has value 1 point over the orange box (only multiplier 2), multiplier mark over the yellow box. (Both multiplication equation 2) The score of this play is equal to [(2×3) +2+2+ (1×2) +2+2+1]×2=17×2=34 points. 2. Various special channels Can only be used for the first overlay. In playing the next time the premium that is overlaid on the special box, then only the premium score will be counted. End Game: 1. The game will end when the player has used all the available pawns. (after all the premiums in the bag) 2. At the same time, the opponent still has the remaining premiums. Find the total score of that premium and multiply it by 2 to add it to the player who has paid all the premium first (except BLANK, do not have to be negative). 3. In the event that both players Can't play the remaining pawns in both of his keys anymore. and told it through three times hold the board and throw it away Counting the scores by subtracting the scores of the remaining pawns in the pad from your own score without multiplying by 2 (BLANK's score is zero).
